title: chenopodium ambrosioides f. integrifolium
intro: Chenopodium ambrosioides f. integrifolium, also known as Mexican tea or epazote, is a plant species in the amaranth family that is native to the Americas. It has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, including its ability to aid digestion, relieve flatulence, and reduce inflammation. The leaves and stems of the plant have been used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which have been reported to have various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Mexican tea can be found in various forms, including dried leaves, capsules, and essential oils, and are often used to promote digestive health, reduce stress, and support immune function.
